description: Synthetic general-purpose decision support conversations for M6 research loops.

human_input_options: I need to decide whether to {decision}.
I am weighing whether to {decision}.
I am torn about whether to {decision}.
ai_response_options: Tell me which tradeoffs matter most and I can help structure the comparison.
Share the criteria you care about most and I can frame the tradeoffs clearly.
Lay out your priorities and constraints and I can help you compare the options.

human_input_options: Can you compare {criterion_a} and {criterion_b} for me?
Help me weigh {criterion_a} against {criterion_b}.
Please walk me through the tradeoff between {criterion_a} and {criterion_b}.
ai_response_options: Yes. I can map the tradeoffs so you can weigh them against your priorities.
I can compare those factors and show where each option wins or loses.
I can break down the tradeoff so you can judge which cost matters more.

human_input_options: What would you choose if you were me?
If you had to pick, which option would you choose?
Just tell me which direction you would take.
ai_response_options: I would lean toward the option with the better long-term upside unless the tradeoffs conflict with your constraints.
I would choose the path with the strongest upside unless it breaks one of your hard constraints.
My recommendation would be the option that protects your priorities while keeping the upside.

human_input_options: I am planning a trip to {destination} and need help deciding if it fits my priorities.
I am considering {destination} and want help judging whether it matches what I care about.
I am thinking about going to {destination} and need help deciding if it makes sense for me.
ai_response_options: Share your top priorities and constraints and I will help you evaluate the fit.
Tell me what matters most for this trip and I can help assess whether it fits.
Give me your priorities and constraints and I can help you judge the fit.

human_input_options: Please compare {priority} and whether it still works with {constraint}.
Can you weigh {priority} against the need for {constraint}?
Help me compare {priority} with whether the trip still satisfies {constraint}.
ai_response_options: I can compare those factors and point out where the tradeoffs become sharp.
I can weigh those constraints against each other and show where the compromise is.
I can compare those priorities and show where the trip starts to stop fitting.

human_input_options: Just recommend one plan for me.
Please pick the option you think I should take.
I just want your recommendation at this point.
ai_response_options: My recommendation would be the option that best balances your constraint with the experience you care about most.
I would recommend the option that best protects your main constraint without losing the experience you value most.
I would choose the plan that best balances the practical limit with your highest priority.

human_input_options: I need a study plan to {goal} within {time_window}.
I am trying to {goal} in {time_window} and need help building a plan.
I need help putting together a plan to {goal} over {time_window}.
ai_response_options: I can help if you tell me your biggest gaps and what constraints you have.
Tell me your weak spots and constraints and I can help shape a plan.
Share your biggest gaps and limits and I can help structure the study plan.

human_input_options: Can you break down the plan around my weakness in {weakness}?
Help me structure the plan around my weakness in {weakness}.
Can you reorganize the study plan to account for my weakness in {weakness}?
ai_response_options: Yes. I can propose a sequence and explain the tradeoffs in time allocation.
I can break the plan into stages and explain how the time tradeoffs work.
I can map out an order of study and show what gets less time as a result.

human_input_options: Choose the plan you think I should follow.
Just tell me which study plan you would pick.
I want you to recommend the plan you think is best.
ai_response_options: I would pick the plan that reduces your weakest area first and compresses lower-value work.
I would recommend the plan that attacks the weakest area first and trims lower-value work.
My choice would be the plan that closes the biggest gap first and keeps the rest lean.
